message:
[medium] asana: reject base_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

validate_base_url_for_auth already pinned the host allowlist for PAT
mode (via base_url_policy) and required https-or-local for credential_id
mode, but returned parsed.to_string() verbatim — query, fragment, and
userinfo components survived into downstream format!("{base_url}/...")
URL construction. A configure payload like
    {"personal_access_token": "pat_x", "base_url":
     "https://app.asana.com/api/1.0?leak=attacker.com"}
would pass validation and then concatenate `?leak=attacker.com` into
every Asana API request (with endpoint path appended to the query).
Userinfo (`https://attacker:pw@app.asana.com/...`) would bake into
every URL and silently override the PAT the connector sets via the
Authorization header.

- validate_base_url_for_auth now rejects userinfo (username or
  password) and query/fragment components before the host-allowlist /
  scheme-allowlist checks, in both PAT and credential_id modes.
- Five new unit tests: PAT reject (query / fragment / userinfo),
  credential_id reject (query), and a clean-happy-path regression.

Verification: `cargo check -p fcp-asana` under CARGO_TARGET_DIR=
/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cannot complete right now because fcp-crypto fails
to build due to a committed syntax-error regression in
crates/fcp-crypto/src/hpke_seal.rs (the `hpke_sealed_box_bytes_roundtrip`
test at :354 has an 8-space-indented `}` at :366 that does not close
the 4-space-indented fn body, leaving the rest of the mod-tests block
unbalanced and line :828's mod-closing `}` flagged as "unexpected
closing delimiter"). That regression is in another pane's domain.
Asana diff reviewed manually: additions are std `url::Url` accessor
calls (`username()`, `password()`, `query()`, `fragment()`) returning
standard shapes, with the same error shape as the surrounding branches
of the validator. Tests use `AsanaAuth::PersonalAccessToken` and
`AsanaAuth::CredentialId` variants verified present at connector.rs:54.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
